content: SHACL validation reports expose sh:focusNode (which instance violated), sh:resultPath, sh:value, sh:sourceShape, sh:resultMessage (human-readable text authored by the shape designer), and sh:resultSeverity (sh:Info/sh:Warning/sh:Violation). UX pattern: renderers map focusNode+resultPath back to the specific form field/UI element and show resultMessage as the user-facing text, using severity to drive icon/color. W3C SHACL 1.2 UI spec formalizes this. Non-expert-facing tools never show raw RDF triples — they translate resultMessage + focusNode into an inline annotation on the offending record.
